name: Is my employer allowed to lower my hourly pay rate while I'm on light duty?

mainEntity.name: Is my employer allowed to lower my hourly pay rate while I'm on light duty?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generally, no. Your employer should not permanently reduce your base pay rate due to a temporary work restriction. If your pay is lower due to different duties, workers' comp should bridge the gap via partial disability benefits.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Employers are generally not allowed to permanently demote you or slash your hourly rate just because you are on light duty. While your 'take-home' pay might change because of fewer hours or different tasks, your base earning capacity should be protected. If your employer uses a light duty assignment as an excuse for a permanent pay cut, it may be considered illegal retaliation or a violation of workers' compensation statutes.

Relevant factors include your employment contract, union agreements, and the laws of the specific state (Utah, Idaho, etc.) regarding wage protection.

Ask for a written explanation of your pay rate for the light duty role. Compare this to the 'Statement of Wages' filed by the insurance company.

When a temporary injury leads to a permanent demotion, it often indicates the employer is trying to force you out, requiring legal protection.
